transcript.text: I keep scrolling through job listings, but it feels like I'm just spinning my wheels. The mortgage is due next week and I can't think about how to secure a new role. The baby's sleep is getting better, but this whole cycle feels endless.

You're facing a really tight deadline with the mortgage due next week, and it's understandable that scrolling through job listings feels like spinning your wheels right now. The baby's sleep getting better is a positive step, but I can see how the overall situation still feels overwhelming. What's the smallest part of this job hunting process that you feel like you could tackle in the next day or two, even if it's just for a few minutes?
I don't even know where to start; maybe I'll just skim a couple of listings for 15 minutes, but honestly I'm stuck and just want to avoid doing anything.

You're feeling really stuck right now, and it's like the pressure of the mortgage deadline is making it hard to think about taking any steps towards job hunting. Even small things, like skimming a couple of job listings, feel like a big commitment. I can see how the fear of not having a good enough resume is holding you back. It might be helpful to break it down into tiny, manageable tasks, like just looking at one job listing for a few minutes, without putting too much pressure on yourself. What do you think is the smallest, most manageable step you could take right now, even if it feels really small?
I'll just open one job listing and read the description for a few minutes—though I'm not even sure that's real progress. The mortgage deadline still feels like a looming cliff edge.

You're considering taking a small step by opening one job listing and reading the description for a few minutes, which might feel like a tiny drop in the ocean, but it's still a start. I can see how the pressure of the mortgage deadline is making it hard to think about taking any steps towards job hunting, and it's understandable that you're unsure if this is real progress. The fact that the baby's sleep is getting better might be a small win to build on. What do you think might happen if you just take this one small step, without putting too much pressure on yourself?
I don't know if opening one listing will actually help, but I guess at least I could see if there's a fit. Still, the mortgage is looming and I feel stuck.

You're feeling really overwhelmed by the pressure of the mortgage deadline and the fear of not having a good enough resume, which is making it hard to think about taking any steps towards job hunting. It's like every small step feels like a huge commitment, and you're unsure if it'll even be helpful. Just acknowledging that it's okay to feel stuck and that taking small steps, like opening one job listing, might be a tiny start, even if it doesn't feel like much. Yeah, it's tough to see right now, but maybe that one small step could lead to something more.

I keep scrolling but it feels like a waste, and I'm terrified that I won't find a good fit before the mortgage is due. The whole thing is a huge life transition and I'm stuck in this cycle of procrastination. It feels like the job search is just another layer of work stress.

You're feeling really stuck right now, and the pressure of the mortgage deadline is weighing heavily on you. It's like every small step in your job search feels like a huge commitment, and you're unsure if it'll even be helpful. I can see how the fear of not having a good enough resume is holding you back. Yeah, it's tough to feel like you're not making progress, especially when it feels like the job search is just another layer of work stress. What's one thing you've been putting off or procrastinating on that you think might help you feel like you're moving forward, even if it's just a tiny step?

I keep putting off updating my résumé, but maybe I could just spend ten minutes reformatting the skills section. That feels like a small step, but I'm still not sure if it's enough to move the job search forward. If I do that, at least I know I'm not staying idle.

You're thinking about spending ten minutes reformatting the skills section of your resume, which feels like a small but potentially doable step. That's a great idea, as it might help you feel like you're making some progress, even if it doesn't feel like a lot. What do you think might happen if you take this small step and then build on it, maybe by doing another small task afterwards? Maybe we can explore how to make this task feel less daunting and more achievable, given your perfectionistic tendencies.
I guess I could do that, but I'm not sure it will actually move anything forward. I still feel like I'm stuck in a loop and don't know if this helps with my career uncertainty.

I can see that you're still feeling really uncertain about whether taking small steps like reformatting your resume will actually move your job search forward. It's like you're trying to find a way to break out of this loop of procrastination and career uncertainty, but it's hard to see what will work. Yeah, it's tough to feel like you're not making progress, especially when the mortgage deadline is looming. Spending ten minutes on your resume's skills section might not feel like a lot, but it's a start, and sometimes that's all we need to begin building momentum.

I guess I could do that ten‑minute reformat, but I'm still not convinced it'll move anything forward.  The mortgage is due next week and my wife is coming back to work, so the pressure is just piling up.

The mortgage deadline and your wife's return to work are really piling up the pressure, and it's understandable that you're feeling overwhelmed. You're considering taking a small step by spending ten minutes reformatting the skills section of your resume, which might not feel like a lot, but it's a start. I can see how the fear of not having a good enough resume is holding you back, but sometimes taking tiny steps can help build momentum. What do you think might make this task feel less daunting and more achievable for you, given your perfectionistic tendencies?

I could try setting a strict 10‑minute timer and then stop, but I'm still not convinced it'll actually push anything forward. The mortgage deadline still feels like a looming cliff, so even small steps feel huge. Still, if I can just get the skills section done, maybe I can at least feel like I’m moving, even if it’s only a tiny bit.

I can see that you're still feeling really uncertain about whether taking small steps like setting a 10-minute timer to work on your resume will actually move your job search forward. The mortgage deadline is looming, and it's understandable that even small steps feel huge right now. However, taking that tiny step, like getting the skills section done, might help you feel like you're moving, even if it's just a tiny bit. What do you think might happen if you take this small step and then build on it, maybe by doing another small task afterwards?

I could set the timer and work on the skills section, but I'm not sure that will actually help me get a job before the mortgage. The deadline feels like a cliff, and even a small task feels huge. If I finish it, I could at least have a part of the resume ready to apply to a few roles.

You're feeling really uncertain about whether taking small steps like setting a timer to work on your resume will actually help you get a job before the mortgage deadline. It's like the deadline feels like a cliff, and even a small task feels huge. However, taking that tiny step, like getting the skills section done, might help you feel like you're moving, even if it's just a tiny bit. What if you did set the timer and work on the skills section - what would that feel like, and how might it help you build momentum?
